posts.author: wartooth6
posts.title: This liberal dumbass in FL running for Congress has been getting trolled relentlessly on his Facebook page lol

posts.raw_content:
Usually, I hear conservatives saying that the problem is that the colleges have been taken over by the wokes. They basically brainwash people in university. But I'm starting to think the problem runs much deeper then that...

College seems to inherently liberalize people's mindsets even if the institution does absolutely nothing to encourage this. One of the biggest effects that going to university does is cause people to delay marriage by approximately 3 years... This is true of every society I have researched, including very conservative ones. It was true even back in the 1950s. College educated individuals married about three years later on average.

The biggest thing that differentiates a liberal's mindset and lifestyle from a conservative's is that a conservative is trying to reach, or already has reached adulthood. Conservatives are trying to get married, own a house, and build a family.

Liberals, on the other hand, want to essentially extend adolescence for as long as possible. Leftists basically want adolescence to be permanent... They want to live for the short term, reduce their responsibilities as much as possible, and never be pressured to settle into a stable pattern.

University inherently encourages this no matter how conservative the culture is. University basically tells people "you should put your life on hold in order to study at this big campus for four years and let the institution basically function like your parents." College, effectively, is high school 2.0. The only difference is that you live there, have the freedom to choose your own classes and schedule. But effectively, it does the same thing that high school does which is delay adulthood for the sake of more formal education.

And the more people go to college, the more that this "extended adolescence" becomes the default of society and everyone starts acting more immature and irresponsible because the college students have normalized it as an expected part of the 18-24 age group. Think about how our society doesn't really treat 18 year olds like adults anymore. The de-facto age of adulthood has become 21. And a small fringe of society is even pushing to make it 25.

This is because of how many people are getting a university education now. Historically, university education was not meant to be something everybody did. It was intended for a very specific, intellectual elite. But nowadays, we treat it almost as expected. I measured the IQ of college graduates compared to non-college graduates and there is barely any difference because a lot of people going to college these days are dumb, and more and more smart people are either not going to college or dropping out.

I would argue that in order to conservatize society, we need to significantly pull back on the cultural norm of mass university attendance. We need to construct a system where your future career and income is no longer dependent or even influenced by whether you get a college education or not. University education should not be a full time pursuit for people doing it either. It should be something more akin to a hobby.

The truth is, any knowledge you want to obtain in this world can be found online. You can ask ChatGPT anything and get an answer that is at least as good as what a college professor would tell you. We have no need to put people in classes for four years while they spend half the time they're there getting drunk and being promiscuous.

posts.author: KuzoKevin
posts.title: The real story about the ICE shooting in Texas -

quotedTweet.content:
Lorenzo Salgado Araujo was a father of three. He lived in Houston for 35 years.

On his way to work, ICE agents shot and killed him. His family learned from a video posted online.

There must be accountability. We need a full, independent investigation.

twitter.content:
James Talirico is a liar.

Five minutes of research into Lorenzo Salgado Araujo would show you that he:

1. Rammed his vehicle into an ICE agents vehicle.
2. Did not follow any commands.
3. Tried to run over ICE agents.

Wake up Texas. This man can’t win in November.
